head: 550c5497b73934bd4268e7bd632f218ded8b21cd
note: Claimed _writable_dir() drops .expanduser(); the call is present verbatim at both heads and no Path(str(configured)) construction exists. Round 2 additionally claimed the PR's own test fails; executed at head: 1 passed. Round 2's body ACKNOWLEDGED the quoted hunk was absent, then re-rationalised and kept `confirmed`.

It escalated major -> blocker.

note: Claimed _writable_dir() drops .expanduser(); the call is present verbatim at both heads and no Path(str(configured)) construction exists. head: d2fbe302665d662dd8207f3b4189128ee5998d78
note: run_store.resume() at L212 precedes edits.prompt validation at L235; resume() also clears pending_step, and L199/L202 then reject the run — unrepairable via the API.

head: d2fbe302665d662dd8207f3b4189128ee5998d78
note: results map only appended to (L134); active filters it out (L141) — a run re-pausing at a downstream gate (which _resume supports) stays hidden and renders stale.

head: d2fbe302665d662dd8207f3b4189128ee5998d78
note: paused_at holds state["pending_step"], a step ID, not a timestamp.

head: cb079fcbd0636c1ffb552b577c8968adf6401377
note: New guard `not str((edits or {}).get('prompt','')).strip()` misses {'prompt': None} because str(None)=='None' is truthy — verified by execution. Finding on a fix the panel itself requested, and correct: the incomplete fix left the orphaning path reachable.

head: cb079fcbd0636c1ffb552b577c8968adf6401377
note: Query-key naming inconsistency. Self-evidencing from the diff; NOT independently grounded.

head: d139f4d0e341664a92eb26a5b2c718c505cece65
note: PASS with zero findings on code unchanged in every relevant respect from cb079fc, where the panel had confirmed the major 8 minutes earlier. New head was pushed BEFORE that FAIL posted, so the author had not seen it. The PASS dismissed the standing block; PR merged 44s later. Defect shipped (protoAgent#2143).

head: 7b1fbb23e205283cc0e8648cf3b720d4d2a7f365
note: Unmount cleanup iterates Object.keys(st.running) only; turn.input_required does markDone (key deleted at 0) then markAwaiting, so awaiting-only entries survive.

head: 7b1fbb23e205283cc0e8648cf3b720d4d2a7f365
note: Missed turn.input_required → started(+1), resumed(+1), usage(-1) leaves a permanent +1. The adjacent code comment claims it 'settles at zero (markDone clamps)' — clamping prevents negatives, not overcounts. Finding contradicts the author's stated reasoning and is right.

head: 7b1fbb23e205283cc0e8648cf3b720d4d2a7f365
note: No FleetActivity unit test anywhere in the tree at that SHA.

head: 7b1fbb23e205283cc0e8648cf3b720d4d2a7f365
note: clearMemberRunning calls clear(), which deletes from running AND awaiting; name/JSDoc say in-flight count only.

head: 7b1fbb23e205283cc0e8648cf3b720d4d2a7f365
note: pickMention strips the @name from the draft (L142); submit() resets to broadcast and returns without clearing it, so the next send broadcasts a message addressed to one member.

head: 4277df44360f6557b5064c46cc84970d70a04580
note: _managed_version() falls back to PYTHON_VERSION when the marker is absent/unreadable, so a marker-less install satisfies the L370 short-circuit and the download is skipped — 'right version installed' concluded from evidence that only says 'some interpreter installed'.

head: 4277df44360f6557b5064c46cc84970d70a04580
note: _install dict mutated off-lock from a worker thread vs event-loop reads. Plausible; proper check requires reasoning about which key combinations can tear. NOT grounded.

disregarded_evidence: Finding 3 in the SAME review states correctly that env_prefix is a strict extension of any_prefix — the reasoning that makes this claim impossible. Verifier confirmed both.
head: fb6b98d5be9fbc70ac43d0a4de5fd67158d5fd72
note: Claimed any_prefix='dev.' falsely matches 'developer.env.TOKEN'; executed: False (4th char is 'e', not '.'). Finding 3 IN THE SAME REVIEW correctly states env_prefix is a strict extension of any_prefix — the reasoning that makes this claim impossible. Verifier confirmed both. NOTE: a real collision exists for dotted delegate names ('dev.foo.env.X'.startswith('dev.') is True) — the finder found the right line and produced an unreal instance of a real class.

head: fb6b98d5be9fbc70ac43d0a4de5fd67158d5fd72
note: _route_secret skips vars neither marked nor is_secretish, while upsert_delegate calls _prune_secrets(name, set(env.keys())) — keep_env keys on PRESENCE, not secret status. So un-toggling never prunes, and merged_delegates overlays the stale secret over the operator's new plaintext on every spawn. Subtle and correct.

head: fb6b98d5be9fbc70ac43d0a4de5fd67158d5fd72
note: env_prefix is a strict extension of any_prefix, so the `or k.startswith(env_prefix)` branch is dead.

head: fb6b98d5be9fbc70ac43d0a4de5fd67158d5fd72
note: assert left["d2.TOKEN" if ... else "d2.env.TOKEN"] asserts truthiness, not == "sk-2"; first branch dead.

head: edf93385706f57644058cdb0ed0a7a74112f50d0
note: except PythonRuntimeError branch returns bare `deps`, dropping already-satisfied optional deps the old code preserved; the success path still preserves them. Regression on one branch only. SHIPPED to main (merged 00:30:27, before the round-2 FAIL posted at 00:33:35). Fix in flight as #2162.

head: edf93385706f57644058cdb0ed0a7a74112f50d0
note: _normalize_dist is private-by-convention but imported cross-module by installer.py.

head: 8880c4d11e6a7caf4d176db402ea8ec4f15f64b7
note: Same major re-reported; still present verbatim at the new head (bare `return deps` at L141 vs preserving success path at L143). Correct carry-forward, NOT re-litigation — contrast #2141 r3.

head: ce627f94fe83a4b17df9c2666e54260529f13ed2
note: Traversal guard is `not str(target).startswith(str(dest) + "/")`. str(Path) yields backslashes on Windows, so every legitimate wheel member raises WheelInstallError there. Zip members always use '/' internally (the endswith check is fine); it is the resolved-Path comparison that is platform-dependent. Frozen DESKTOP app — Windows is in scope.

head: ce627f94fe83a4b17df9c2666e54260529f13ed2
note: _record_lock writes {plugin_id: {...}} as a top-level key; installer._write_lock does data['plugins'].sort(...), expecting {'plugins': [...]}. Two writers of the same lock_path() with incompatible schemas.
